title: podman failed to login with error `default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument`
body:
### Issue Description

Working with Fedora40 with `podman-5:5.2.3-1.fc40.x86_64` (was recently updated), running with Jenkins pipeline we have a login step that constantly fails with the following error
```
14:05:38  + podman login docker.io --username **** --password ****
14:05:38  time="2024-10-27T14:05:37+02:00" level=warning msg="RunRoot is pointing to a path (/run/user/1000/containers) which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail."
14:05:38  Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument
```

Downgrading podman to `podman-5:5.0.0-1.fc40.x86_64` seems to solve the issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. 
2.
3.

I couldn't reproduce it manually, only via the Jenkins pipeline

### Describe the results you received

The login failed with the mentioned error

### Describe the results you expected

Be able to login

comments.body: Something with your IDs? The error you point out rundir is looking in user 1000 and in podman info it is looking into user 0.  Is this being done with su - or sudo?

As mentioned error like this generally mean inproper user session setups, using su/sudo to switch users will not create proper systemd user session that we require for proper function.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#31-podman-run-fails-with-erro0000-xdg_runtime_dir-directory-runuser0-is-not-owned-by-the-current-user-or-error-creating-tmpdir-mkdir-runuser1000-permission-denied

also see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22503 which seems to suggest the problem ws caused by the missing login session 

The error in particular seems odd because `default OCI runtime "crun" not found` mean it suddenly cannot no longer find crun, are you messing around with $PATH or other paths on the fs?  In any case I doubt this is actually a podman bug.
Thanks for your feedback, i will check it
